items.title: Best of the Worst: Star Slammer, Talos, and The Apple
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content:
<article>Ahoy! It’s time for an epic Best of the Worst episode filled with magic, martial arts, and Menahem! Menahem Golan that is, of Cannon Films, as we celebrate the sci fi dystopian musical The Apple! We also watched some disgusting trash called Star Slammer about a women’s prison in space. It’s filth. Utter filth. What would Jesus do? And to round things out, we watch the ambitious no budget martial arts fantasy adventure film Talos. Look out everyone, it’s Talos! 

Special thanks to Bear McCreary (https://bearmccreary.com) for joining us on this nightmare.</article>

items.title: When an Actress Stopped Playing It Safe – Emma Stone
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
<article>In the early 2010s, Emma Stone was everywhere. With Superbad, Zombieland, and Easy A, she became the poster girl for the witty love interest or quirky lead, continuing to similar roles like in Crazy Stupid Love and The Amazing Spider-Man. But for her, that wasn’t enough.

The roles kept coming, but not the chances to grow. So instead of playing it safe, she chased tougher roles like in The Help and Birdman, with one even leaving her wondering if she was even good enough. But she didn’t back down. She pushed further, taking on live theatre, which became the hardest thing she’s ever done.

Those risks laid the foundation for La La Land, a turning point that changed how the industry saw her. And instead of coasting after that, she doubled down by choosing weirder, bolder films. The Favourite. Cruella. Poor Things. Kinds of Kindness. Bugonia. Not just acting in them, but producing them too.

This is the story of an actress who refuses to settle, and quietly built one of the most exciting careers in Hollywood. This is Emma Stone.

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
00:45 - Project Hollywood
04:20 - The Shift
08:48 - No More Playing It Safe
13:27 - In Full Control

Emma Stone. Superbad. The Rocker. The House Bunny. Zombieland. Jonah Hill. Jesse Eisenberg. Easy A. Crazy Stupid Love. The Help. The Amazing Spiderman. Andrew Garfield. Gwen Stacy. The Croods. Birdman. Aloha. Woody Allen. La La Land. Damien Chazelle. Billie Jean King. Yorgos Lanthimos. The Favourite. Cruella. Poor Things. Kinds of Kindness. Eddington. Ari Aster. Bugonia. 

If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.

If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.

Patreon: https://patreon.com/FilmStack
Twitter: https://twitter.com/realFilmStack
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/

#emmastone    #actress    #lalaland</article>

items.title: Half in the Bag: Superman (2025)
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content: <article>Have you ever heard of Superman? Have you ever seen a Superman movie? Well, there's another one now. It's the newest one. There have been Superman movies and now this is one of them. This one is directed by James Gunn. The previous one was directed by Zack Snyder. The two of them worked together a long time ago on the Dawn of the Dead remake. The original Dawn of the Dead was filmed in Pittsburgh. Pittsburgh is the second most populated city in the state of Pennsylvania. It is commonly knows as the Steel City. Superman is often called the Man of Steel. Man of Steel is a movie that was directed by Zack Snyder. But this new Superman movie is not directed by Zack Snyder.</article>

items.title: The Story of Superman: The Movie (1978)
items.author: Layton Eversaul
items.content:
<article>In this in-depth and feature-length retrospective, I tell the story behind cinema's first blockbuster comic book movie: "Superman: The Movie" (1978). From its shaky development to its innovated but troubled production, from its massive marketing campaign to its generation-spanning legacy, I reveal the true story behind the making of a groundbreaking classic. Directed by Richard Donner. Starring: Christopher Reeve, Margot Kidder, Marlon Brando, and Gene Hackman. 

NOW ON PATREON: https://www.patreon.com/LaytonEversaul
